title: hibiscus moscheutos subsp. lasiocarpos
intro: Hibiscus moscheutos subsp. lasiocarpos, also known as the swamp rose-mallow or marsh mallow, is a perennial plant belonging to the mallow family (Malvaceae), native to eastern North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of mucilages that soothe and calm irritated tissues. The health benefits of hibiscus moscheutos subsp. lasiocarpos include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from digestive issues. Hibiscus moscheutos subsp. lasiocarpos can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or poultice, and is sometimes used in traditional herbal remedies for its various medicinal properties.
